chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The host introduces the concept of an animal-based diet, highlighting its benefits such as improving strength, health, and possibly aiding in weight loss and autoimmune conditions. They suggest that this diet aligns with human eating habits for thousands of years and serve as a good starting point.
chunks.transcript: so many of you guys said that you shop at Aldi so I'm going to take you with me we're going to do an Aldi grocery haul animal based style let's go I'm going to show you all the foods that I think are the best in there so we're doing animal base we got meat we're going to look for fruit we're going to look for some honey and we're going to look for the best dairy in there there's no raw dairy in Aldi but we're going to try and find the best stuff I'm a huge fan of the animal- based diet I think it's a great simple way to start your diet if you're looking to get stronger feel better potentially fix autoimmune conditions or lose weight it's a good blueprint to start with it doesn't have to be exactly how you eat but I think an animal-based diet is very consistent with the way that humans have eaten for hundreds of thousands of years and it's a good starting point for you so come on let me show you how I would

chunks.title: Shopping for Meat at Aldi
chunks.summary: The host discusses different options for meat available at Aldi, focusing on grass-fed beef as the preferred choice. They compare prices and quality between grass-fed options and more affordable factory-farmed products, noting the nutritional benefits of grass-fed meat.
chunks.transcript: shop animal-based at ald okay my first thought is that this card is freaking huge I never need something this big let's go get meat first that's where I start at the grocery store chunks.title: Evaluating Chicken and Fish Options
chunks.summary: Chicken options at Aldi are reviewed, with a preference for free-range organic chicken despite some concerns about the saline solution used in processing. The segment also critiques Atlantic salmon versus wild-caught salmon, warning about the differences in farming and nutrient content.
chunks.transcript: they have chicken here I'm not as excited about chicken but if you wanted to get chicken you could get free range organic chicken this is $5 a pound so still affordable for free range organic chicken you'll notice on the label that it says it has up to 5% retained water which means they kind of like wash it in a saline solution to make it plump up a little bit to increase the price to increase kind of the weight of it but still I mean in terms of chicken this is the best option here it's not my favorite choice I think if a cow is eating grass all of its life that's a better meat to eat than chicken which is going to be eating grains this chicken is eating organic grains but it still has a saline solution in here it's not my favorite but it's not Chick-fil-A it's way better than Chick-fil-A for sure so even at Aldi they've got pretty affordable organic chicken breasts $5 a pound let's take a look at the fish so they have Alaskan wild caught sockeye salmon not a bad option in terms of fish you want to be pretty careful though because Atlantic salmon is farm raised all of the time there is no such thing as wild Atlantic salmon and if you look at the labeling on a Atlantic salmon you're going to to see coloring you're going to see they actually add colors because if they didn't add color to that fish it would be white it would look like this like tilapia that's how farm rais salmon looks if they don't actually have coloring added to it so understand that this Atlantic salmon has coloring added through the feed and an Atlantic

chunks.title: Cheese and Dairy Products
chunks.summary: The host highlights the availability of raw cheese and grass-fed cheddar at Aldi, discussing the importance of raw dairy. They also mention options for different types of butter available in the store.
chunks.transcript: salmon is a farm ra salmon it's not a wild salmon it's basically similar to grass-fed versus grain fed cows this one ingredients just salmon all right guys so I found one raw cheese at Aldi which is awesome it's parmesano regano parmesano regano is always raw all right guys so it's just happening more and more at grocery stores we're getting asked to not film I don't understand why this is but I got some cool stuff in the cart I'm going to try and show you guys what I've got if we get kicked out we get kicked out but I did the rest of my grocery Hol at all and I'll show you what I got here so we talked about the raw cheese parmesano regano is always raw so you can see on the label it says milk salt and renit and that is a raw cheese always they also have a grass-fed cheddar cheese which is not raw and that's okay it's not ideal but they at least have one raw cheese I've got two different types of butter here which are kind of cool you got an organic butter at Aldi and you've got an Irish butter which is GRA grassfed I would probably opt for the grass-fed butter rather than the organic butter but they're both good options in

chunks.title: Selecting Fruits and Organic Produce
chunks.summary: The host expresses disappointment about the lack of organic produce at Aldi but shares their selections of non-organic fruits. They introduce a method using baking soda to clean conventional produce, effectively reducing pesticide residue.
chunks.transcript: terms of fruit they have zero organic produce here at Aldi which is a bummer but I got dates I got oranges I got cherries and I got strawberries and what I also got was baking soda so I don't know if you guys I can't have any filming in the store about it but if you're not going to stop I will have to ask for and I don't want to do that we'll stop we'll stop we're done yeah [Music] yeah all right guys so shut down at Aldi basically kicked out but I did find some interesting stuff in there I was talking about the baking soda when the manager gets mad at us again yeah so I would use baking soda to clean the fruit that you're getting at places like this if you can't find organic fruit use baking soda I've done short form content about baking soda it actually works you can take the produce and what you do is you soak it in a 1% solution of baking soda and it removes a significant amount of the pesticides the idea is that baking soda is alkaline a lot of these pesticides are acidic so there's some sort of a neutralization going on here I've actually used pesticide detection strips to prove this so if you can only get conventional produce if you can't get organic just buy some baking soda

chunks.title: Egg Choices and Conclusion
chunks.summary: The host compares the merits of organic cage-free eggs versus pasture-raised eggs, emphasizing the benefits of pasture-raised options for better animal welfare and nutrient access. They wrap up by summarizing their overall experience shopping for an animal-based diet at Aldi.
chunks.transcript: soak it in a 1% solution which is 2 teaspoons per liter for 15 minutes rinse it off and it will significantly reduce the pesticides I talked about the cheese they have a few different types of of honey here they have local and they have a raw honey not the best options they're all in plastic but you can find decent stuff here at Aldi and in terms of eggs the two different types of eggs I found were organic cagefree and non-organic pasture raised it's like a tossup pasture raised chickens are going to have more room to roam they have about 108 ft to walk around they're going to get more bugs and worms cagefree chickens they get maybe I think it's like six or seven feet per chicken so they don't have as much room to roam but they're fed organic grains the pasture race chickens are definitely fed grains which are not going to be organic so it's a tossup I'd probably go for the pasture raised rather than the cage free organic just like I would go for the grass-fed butter versus the organic butter that's not grass-fed there's a few toss-ups here at Aldi but in terms of affordability they've got decent stuff at reasonable prices so I don't know it always bums me out when we get kids out of these places but at least you guys can get a sense of what I would buy at Aldi the best things in the store I put them in the cart we got some video of it I couldn't talk about all of it because we tried twice guys I'm just a rule breaker but the manager was like you out you got to get out of here so that's what I would get at Aldi hopefully that's helpful for you guys and I will keep doing these grocery hauls let me know where you like to shop I will show you what I would buy there I will show you the best things that I can find in those stores hopefully that's helpful for you so you can make better choices in your diet and you can be healthier I'll see you guys in the next
